prompt: Create a 12-second Manim scene that explains a 2D rotation matrix by showing the standard basis vectors and their rotated images. Keep the focus on how the matrix columns become the new basis directions.
completion:
from manim import *

class MatrixRotationColumnsStory(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        title = Text('Matrix Columns as New Basis Directions', font_size=32).to_edge(UP)
        plane = NumberPlane(x_range=[-3, 3, 1], y_range=[-3, 3, 1], x_length=6.2, y_length=6.2, background_line_style={'stroke_opacity': 0.18}).shift(LEFT * 1.1 + DOWN * 0.2)
        e1 = Arrow(plane.c2p(0, 0), plane.c2p(1, 0), buff=0, color=BLUE_D)
        e2 = Arrow(plane.c2p(0, 0), plane.c2p(0, 1), buff=0, color=GREEN_D)
        e1_label = Text('e1', font_size=22, color=BLUE_D).next_to(e1.get_end(), RIGHT, buff=0.12)
        e2_label = Text('e2', font_size=22, color=GREEN_D).next_to(e2.get_end(), UP, buff=0.12)
        r1 = Arrow(plane.c2p(0, 0), plane.c2p(0.7, 0.7), buff=0, color=BLUE_D)
        r2 = Arrow(plane.c2p(0, 0), plane.c2p(-0.7, 0.7), buff=0, color=GREEN_D)
        r1_label = Text('col 1', font_size=22, color=BLUE_D).next_to(r1.get_end(), RIGHT, buff=0.12)
        r2_label = Text('col 2', font_size=22, color=GREEN_D).next_to(r2.get_end(), LEFT, buff=0.12)
        matrix = MathTex(r'R = \begin{bmatrix} \cos \theta & -\sin \theta \\ \sin \theta & \cos \theta \end{bmatrix}', font_size=34).to_corner(UR).shift(DOWN * 0.65)
        caption = Text('The transformed basis vectors are the columns of the matrix.', font_size=22, color=WHITE).to_edge(DOWN)

        self.play(FadeIn(title), Create(plane), run_time=2)
        self.play(GrowArrow(e1), GrowArrow(e2), FadeIn(e1_label), FadeIn(e2_label), run_time=2)
        self.play(ReplacementTransform(e1, r1), ReplacementTransform(e2, r2), Transform(e1_label, r1_label), Transform(e2_label, r2_label), FadeIn(matrix), run_time=3)
        self.play(FadeIn(caption), Indicate(VGroup(r1, r2), color=YELLOW_A), run_time=2)
        self.wait(1)

prompt: Create a 14-second Manim scene that explains heap insertion. Show a small binary heap, insert a new value at the next leaf, then animate the value bubbling upward through two swaps until the heap property is restored.
completion:
from manim import *

class HeapInsertBubbleUpStory(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        title = Text('Heap Insert Uses Bubble-Up', font_size=34).to_edge(UP)
        positions = {'50': UP * 1.5, '35': LEFT * 2.0 + UP * 0.2, '28': RIGHT * 2.0 + UP * 0.2, '20': LEFT * 2.8 + DOWN * 1.1, '18': LEFT * 1.2 + DOWN * 1.1, 'new': RIGHT * 1.2 + DOWN * 1.1, '14': RIGHT * 2.8 + DOWN * 1.1}
        def node(value, pos, color=BLUE_D):
            circle = Circle(radius=0.34, color=color, fill_color=color, fill_opacity=0.18)
            label = Integer(value, font_size=24).move_to(circle)
            return VGroup(circle, label).move_to(pos)
        nodes = {
            '50': node(50, positions['50']),
            '35': node(35, positions['35']),
            '28': node(28, positions['28']),
            '20': node(20, positions['20']),
            '18': node(18, positions['18']),
            '14': node(14, positions['14']),
        }
        new_node = node(42, positions['new'], YELLOW_D)
        edges = VGroup(
            Line(nodes['50'].get_center(), nodes['35'].get_center(), color=GRAY_B),
            Line(nodes['50'].get_center(), nodes['28'].get_center(), color=GRAY_B),
            Line(nodes['35'].get_center(), nodes['20'].get_center(), color=GRAY_B),
            Line(nodes['35'].get_center(), nodes['18'].get_center(), color=GRAY_B),
            Line(nodes['28'].get_center(), positions['new'], color=GRAY_B),
            Line(nodes['28'].get_center(), nodes['14'].get_center(), color=GRAY_B),
        )
        heap_group = VGroup(*nodes.values(), new_node, edges)
        caption_one = Text('Insert at the next open leaf position.', font_size=22, color=WHITE).to_edge(DOWN)
        caption_two = Text('If the new key is larger than its parent, swap upward.', font_size=22, color=WHITE).to_edge(DOWN)
        caption_three = Text('Stop when the heap property is restored.', font_size=22, color=WHITE).to_edge(DOWN)

        self.play(FadeIn(title), Create(edges), *[FadeIn(node_group) for node_group in nodes.values()], run_time=2.5)
        self.play(FadeIn(new_node), FadeIn(caption_one), run_time=1.5)
        self.play(new_node.animate.move_to(positions['28']), nodes['28'].animate.move_to(positions['new']), Transform(caption_one, caption_two), run_time=2.5)
        self.play(new_node.animate.move_to(positions['50']), nodes['50'].animate.move_to(positions['28']), Transform(caption_one, caption_three), run_time=2.5)
        self.wait(1)

prompt: Create a 12-second Manim scene that explains why pressure increases with depth in a fluid. Show a water column, three points at different depths, and a simple readout indicating low, medium, and high pressure as the depth increases downward.
completion:
from manim import *

class PressureDepthGaugeStory(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        title = Text('Pressure Increases with Depth', font_size=34).to_edge(UP)
        tank = Rectangle(width=4.2, height=4.8, color=BLUE_D, fill_color=BLUE_E, fill_opacity=0.16).shift(DOWN * 0.15)
        surface = Line(LEFT * 2.1 + UP * 2.25, RIGHT * 2.1 + UP * 2.25, color=TEAL_D, stroke_width=4).shift(DOWN * 0.15)
        points = VGroup(Dot(LEFT * 0.6 + UP * 1.4, color=YELLOW_D, radius=0.08), Dot(LEFT * 0.2 + DOWN * 0.1, color=YELLOW_D, radius=0.08), Dot(RIGHT * 0.3 + DOWN * 1.6, color=YELLOW_D, radius=0.08))
        guides = VGroup(*[DashedLine(point.get_center(), RIGHT * 3.0 + np.array([0, point.get_y(), 0]), color=GRAY_B) for point in points])
        labels = VGroup(Text('low', font_size=22, color=GREEN_D).move_to(RIGHT * 3.6 + UP * 1.4), Text('medium', font_size=22, color=YELLOW_D).move_to(RIGHT * 3.8 + DOWN * 0.1), Text('high', font_size=22, color=RED_D).move_to(RIGHT * 3.6 + DOWN * 1.6))
        caption = Text('More fluid sits above deeper points, so the pressure there is higher.', font_size=22, color=WHITE).to_edge(DOWN)

        self.play(FadeIn(title), FadeIn(tank), Create(surface), run_time=2)
        self.play(FadeIn(points), run_time=1)
        self.play(LaggedStart(*[Create(line) for line in guides], lag_ratio=0.2), LaggedStart(*[FadeIn(label) for label in labels], lag_ratio=0.2), run_time=3)
        self.play(FadeIn(caption), Indicate(points[2], color=RED_A), run_time=2)
        self.wait(1)

prompt: Create a 12-second Manim scene that explains enzyme-substrate fit. Show a simplified enzyme pocket, a matching substrate approaching, a short bound state, and labels that make the lock-and-key idea clear without overloading the frame.
completion:
from manim import *

class EnzymeFitStory(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        title = Text('Enzyme and Substrate Fit', font_size=34).to_edge(UP)
        enzyme = Difference(Circle(radius=1.45, color=BLUE_D, fill_color=BLUE_E, fill_opacity=0.22), RoundedRectangle(corner_radius=0.12, width=0.9, height=0.65, color=BLUE_D).shift(RIGHT * 0.95), color=BLUE_D, fill_color=BLUE_E, fill_opacity=0.22).shift(LEFT * 1.2 + DOWN * 0.1)
        substrate = RoundedRectangle(corner_radius=0.12, width=0.82, height=0.55, color=YELLOW_D, fill_color=YELLOW_D, fill_opacity=0.35).shift(RIGHT * 3.0 + DOWN * 0.1)
        enzyme_label = Text('enzyme', font_size=22, color=BLUE_D).next_to(enzyme, DOWN, buff=0.2)
        substrate_label = Text('substrate', font_size=22, color=YELLOW_D).next_to(substrate, DOWN, buff=0.2)
        fit_label = Text('matching shape', font_size=24, color=GREEN_D).move_to(RIGHT * 2.5 + UP * 1.4)
        bound_note = Text('temporary complex', font_size=24, color=GREEN_D).next_to(enzyme, UP, buff=0.3)
        caption = Text('A matching substrate can fit into the active site and bind briefly.', font_size=22, color=WHITE).to_edge(DOWN)

        self.play(FadeIn(title), FadeIn(enzyme), FadeIn(enzyme_label), FadeIn(substrate), FadeIn(substrate_label), run_time=2)
        self.play(FadeIn(fit_label), run_time=1)
        self.play(substrate.animate.move_to(LEFT * 0.3 + DOWN * 0.1), substrate_label.animate.next_to(LEFT * 0.3 + DOWN * 0.1, DOWN, buff=0.22), run_time=2.5)
        self.play(FadeOut(fit_label), FadeIn(bound_note), FadeIn(caption), run_time=1.5)
        self.wait(1)

prompt: Create a 12-second Manim scene that explains why larger samples can produce narrower confidence intervals. Show three horizontal intervals for small, medium, and large sample sizes centered on the same estimate, with visibly shrinking widths.
completion:
from manim import *

class ConfidenceIntervalShrinkStory(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        title = Text('Larger Samples Narrow the Interval', font_size=34).to_edge(UP)
        axis = NumberLine(x_range=[0, 10, 1], length=8.2, include_numbers=False).shift(DOWN * 0.2)
        center = axis.n2p(5)
        lines = VGroup(
            Line(axis.n2p(2.4), axis.n2p(7.6), color=RED_D, stroke_width=8).shift(UP * 1.2),
            Line(axis.n2p(3.3), axis.n2p(6.7), color=YELLOW_D, stroke_width=8),
            Line(axis.n2p(4.0), axis.n2p(6.0), color=GREEN_D, stroke_width=8).shift(DOWN * 1.2),
        )
        ticks = VGroup(*[Line(center + direction * 0.0 + DOWN * 0.18, center + UP * 0.18, color=GRAY_B, stroke_width=4) for direction in [LEFT, ORIGIN, RIGHT]])
        labels = VGroup(Text('small n', font_size=22, color=RED_D).next_to(lines[0], LEFT, buff=0.25), Text('medium n', font_size=22, color=YELLOW_D).next_to(lines[1], LEFT, buff=0.25), Text('large n', font_size=22, color=GREEN_D).next_to(lines[2], LEFT, buff=0.25))
        estimate = Dot(center, color=BLUE_D, radius=0.08)
        estimate_label = Text('same estimate', font_size=22, color=BLUE_D).next_to(estimate, UP, buff=0.18)
        caption = Text('More data can reduce uncertainty even when the central estimate stays the same.', font_size=22, color=WHITE).to_edge(DOWN)

        self.play(FadeIn(title), Create(axis), FadeIn(estimate), FadeIn(estimate_label), run_time=2)
        self.play(LaggedStart(*[Create(line) for line in lines], lag_ratio=0.25), LaggedStart(*[FadeIn(label) for label in labels], lag_ratio=0.25), run_time=3)
        self.play(FadeIn(caption), Indicate(lines[2], color=GREEN_A), run_time=2)
        self.wait(1)

prompt: Create a 14-second Manim scene that explains seasons using Earth's axial tilt. Show the Sun, a tilted Earth at two orbital positions, and simple sunlight arrows so it is clear why one hemisphere receives more direct light in one position and less in the opposite position.
completion:
from manim import *

class SeasonsTiltStory(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        title = Text('Seasons Come from Tilt, Not Distance', font_size=32).to_edge(UP)
        sun = Circle(radius=0.85, color=ORANGE, fill_color=ORANGE, fill_opacity=0.34).shift(LEFT * 4.0)
        sun_label = Text('Sun', font_size=22, color=ORANGE).next_to(sun, DOWN, buff=0.16)
        earth_left = Circle(radius=0.38, color=BLUE_D, fill_color=BLUE_E, fill_opacity=0.34).shift(RIGHT * 0.8 + UP * 1.2)
        earth_right = Circle(radius=0.38, color=BLUE_D, fill_color=BLUE_E, fill_opacity=0.34).shift(RIGHT * 3.2 + DOWN * 1.2)
        tilt_left = Line(earth_left.get_center() + DOWN * 0.55 + LEFT * 0.15, earth_left.get_center() + UP * 0.55 + RIGHT * 0.15, color=YELLOW_D, stroke_width=4)
        tilt_right = Line(earth_right.get_center() + DOWN * 0.55 + LEFT * 0.15, earth_right.get_center() + UP * 0.55 + RIGHT * 0.15, color=YELLOW_D, stroke_width=4)
        arrows_left = VGroup(*[Arrow(sun.get_right() + UP * offset, earth_left.get_left() + UP * offset * 0.2, color=ORANGE, buff=0.0, stroke_width=3, max_tip_length_to_length_ratio=0.14) for offset in [-0.5, 0.0, 0.5]])
        arrows_right = VGroup(*[Arrow(sun.get_right() + UP * offset, earth_right.get_left() + UP * offset * 0.2, color=ORANGE, buff=0.0, stroke_width=3, max_tip_length_to_length_ratio=0.14) for offset in [-0.5, 0.0, 0.5]])
        summer_note = Text('north tilted toward Sun', font_size=22, color=GREEN_D).next_to(earth_left, RIGHT, buff=0.28)
        winter_note = Text('north tilted away', font_size=22, color=RED_D).next_to(earth_right, RIGHT, buff=0.28)
        orbit_arc = ArcBetweenPoints(earth_left.get_center(), earth_right.get_center(), angle=-PI / 3, color=GRAY_B)
        caption = Text('The tilt changes the light angle across the year, creating opposite seasons.', font_size=22, color=WHITE).to_edge(DOWN)

        self.play(FadeIn(title), FadeIn(sun), FadeIn(sun_label), run_time=2)
        self.play(FadeIn(earth_left), Create(tilt_left), FadeIn(arrows_left), FadeIn(summer_note), run_time=2)
        self.play(Create(orbit_arc), FadeIn(earth_right), Create(tilt_right), FadeIn(arrows_right), FadeIn(winter_note), run_time=3)
        self.play(FadeIn(caption), run_time=1.5)
        self.wait(1)
